items.title: 2 Button Dragonhunter Power 34.6k Radiance
items.rotation: Don't cancel auto attack chains. Cast Procession of Blades and F1 between auto attack chains.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:18 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
So, just to explain the build, all you really want to do is make sure that you don't cancel your auto-attack chain, aka make sure that you hit your third auto-attack and put down the Symbol of Protection. Your opener is Procession of Blades into your F1. Basically, you want to cast Procession of Blades and F1 in between your auto-attack chains. If both are up, make sure you cast Procession of Blades first. If you're watching the three-button video, Test of Faith has no cast time, so you can cast it whenever. You won't interrupt your auto-attack chain. If you're watching the two-button video, this is Signet of Wrath, so you can ignore that.

As for some of the useful skills, Hammer 2 allows you to get around. Hammer 4 is a CC. Hammer 5 I think technically is also a CC, but I think an enemy has to pass through it, so probably not as useful. Technically, F1 pull is also a CC, like if you recast F1, but that will hurt your damage. F2 is a nice skill to get around. F3 lets you block basically any attacks in front of you, so maybe something nice if you feel like there's a lot of damage pressure.

Your heal skill lets you heal for a percentage of the damage dealt. So, for example, if you are in a fight and you're running low, or if you're doing Gorseval or something and you're about to take a lot of damage, you can potentially pop this and you will probably survive whatever is coming. A nice thing about using a heal skill is you gain resolution, and as part of getting resolution it increases your chance to critically strike. So, for example, if you're in a group where resolution is low, this is just nice to spam off cooldown anyway.

Your elite you can replace with Dragon's Maw for extra CC. Otherwise, you can leave Signet of Courage if you don't plan to use more than two or three buttons. Bane Signet is a CC if you use it, but it basically just gives you power. Guardian also has a lot of really nice utility. Judge's Intervention lets you blink to an enemy target. Merciful Intervention lets you blink to an ally target. Wall of Reflection lets you reflect. Sanctuary is a really nice CC skill and projectile destruction skill. Basically, if you put it down on an opponent, it will pulse and do defiance bar damage over and over again. Hallowed Ground and Stand Your Ground! are both nice stability skills. Technically you could take Hammer of Wisdom if you're really lacking CC, but hopefully that's not the case.

If you do decide that you want to run extra CC or swap one of these utility skills, Test of Faith is the skill to replace. That's it. I hope you enjoy the build.

2:14 Rundown

items.title: 3 Button Dragonhunter Power 37.9k Virtues Realistic
items.rotation: Don't cancel auto attack chains. Cast Procession of Blades and F1 between auto attack chains. Cast Test of Faith (this has no cast time) off cooldown.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Realistic == 10 boons. You have traits that scale with number of boons on you.

0:00 Rotation
2:06 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, all you want to do is time your Procession of Blades with your F1. Because you're taking Virtues, your F1 is going to be on a lower cooldown than usual, so it will time really nicely with Procession of Blades. You want to cast your Test of Faith off cooldown whenever it's up. One thing I want to talk about with Virtues is the trait Unscathed Contender. This trait is slightly unrealistic depending on the fight you're in. If you read it, you get a 10% damage increase with Aegis and a 7% damage increase while your health is above 90%. Basically, if the damage pressure is really low, your Aegis uptime is really high, and you have high health most of the time, then Virtues is going to be stronger to run than Radiance. However, if you're playing it on a fight where there's a lot of damage pressure, your Aegis uptime is low, you're taking a lot of damage, your health is bouncing frequently, and your Unscathed Contender uptime is low, then I'd likely recommend you take the Radiance trait line instead. The problem with the Radiance trait line is Righteous Instincts. Resolution increases your chance to critically strike, so you basically need high Resolution uptime for Radiance to work, unlike Virtues. The nice thing is that with Radiance you can take full Berserker's and you don't have to run the weird Assassin's mix that this build runs. So I'll leave it up to you on what you want to do. For example, if you're playing this build on Mursaat Overseer, Virtues is going to be stronger to run. But if you're playing it on something like Sabetha, where there are a lot of adds, they're popping your Aegis, and there's a lot of damage pressure, maybe Virtues is not worth running. That's the only thing I wanted to call out for Virtues.

Otherwise, I think it's a pretty solid build. Hammer 4 is a CC. Recasting your F1 is a CC. F2 is a nice mobility skill. F3 will give you Aegis from the front, so if there's damage pressure coming in you can pop F3. Your heal skill will heal you for 33% of your damage dealt, so it can be nice to pop if you're taking a lot of damage and want to keep your Unscathed Contender uptime high. Your Bane Signet is a CC, although I would try to save it as much as you can because once you pop it your extra power will go away. Your elite can be replaced with Dragon's Maw.

If you're going to replace anything in this build, replace Test of Faith and just play it as a two-button build. Some useful skills to replace it with are Judge's Intervention, a blink to an enemy; Merciful Intervention, a blink to an ally; Wall of Reflection, a reflect wall; Hold the Line! and Stand Your Ground!, which you can consider as stability options and also for Resolution if you decide to run Radiance; Sanctuary, which is really strong CC; and Hammer of Wisdom, which is technically also a CC.

Keep in mind the difference between Radiance and Virtues and look at the fight that you're in. I think both are viable. It just depends on the situation. Thank you.

items.title: 3 Button Firebrand Condi 31.9k
items.rotation: Basically spam mantra of flame off cooldown. Fit in axe 2 and torch 4 after an axe auto attack. To make it simplest you can align axe 2 torch 4 casts to be synced like I did.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

0:00 Rotation
2:24 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
So, to explain the Firebrand builds, you basically just want to cast either skill 2, if you're running the three-button build, which is Symbol of Vengeance, or Purging Flames after an auto attack. You can time your Zealot's Flame either right after you cast Symbol of Vengeance or right after you cast Purging Flames. Your Mantra of Flame you can just dump off cooldown. If you're running the four-button build, Purging Flames is your highest priority. If not, then all you really do with the three-button build is spam Symbol of Vengeance.

The thing about these Firebrand builds is that since they don't use tomes, all of your tomes are free to be used as you wish. I would recommend taking some time to read through what your tome skills do. Some notable ones: skill 3 in Tome of Justice is a pull. Skills 4 and 5 are big AoE damage. This applies Ashes of the Just to all of your allies, so it basically just increases your damage. Tome of Resolve has some notable ones too: skill 4 gives some healing, and skill 5 converts conditions to boons, which is a really nice condition cleanse. If your team is getting nuked with conditions, you can just go into Tome of Resolve, pop 5, and get out. Tome of Resolve skill 2 also removes some conditions.

Then the big one is Tome of Courage. This is why I think this Firebrand build is pretty good. Skill 3 is a reflect, which is really strong on a lot of encounters. Make sure that you know this exists. Skill 5 is just big protection. For you as a DPS, the most notable skill here is the reflect dome in Tome of Courage.

Some useful skills: Litany of Wrath will heal you. This does apply to both strike and condition damage, so it's a useful healing skill for you to run. Mantra of Solace for the most part isn't super useful for you as a DPS, so you can most likely just ignore it. For your elite, Mantra of Liberation is worth considering if you're willing to press it, since it's a stun break and stability for you and your team.

Some other notable Guardian skills are Judge's Intervention, which is a blink to an enemy; Merciful Intervention, which is a blink to an ally; Wall of Reflection, another reflection option, although you'd probably prefer the Tome of Courage one; Hallowed Ground and Stand Your Ground!, which are both good stability options, though you should probably prefer your elite Mantra of Liberation; Sanctuary, which is a really strong CC option; and Hammer of Wisdom if you want some extra CC. Guardian has a lot of good utility.

That's it. Enjoy the build.

items.title: 4 Button Firebrand Quickness Healer
items.rotation: Use heal, Mantra of Potence, Advance, Feel my Wrath off cooldown.
items.explanation:
This is a beginner/low intensity guardian build for fractal/raid/strike content.

Also I forgot to mention but if you run staff you can swap out Advance to something else. Or if you decide to use your tome skills you can also swap out Advance if you are granting swiftness. But you do want to make sure you are granting swiftness so use advance if you don't use staff or tomes.

0:00 Rotation
1:15 Rundown
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, the build is pretty simple. All you really want to do is spam your heal, Mantra of Potence, Advance!, and Feel My Wrath! off cooldown and auto-attack with hammer. It's hard for me to make these low-intensity healer builds because I know that they won't work everywhere, but I try to focus on giving you as much passive healing as I can. You don't need to press your buttons for the most part, but there will be fights where you need burst healing.

If you can, please run staff. Staff is really, really good. Staff 3, Staff 4, and Staff 2 are all really good skills. Unfortunately, staff doesn't have as much passive healing as hammer does, so I still presented hammer, but I would seriously recommend running staff.

Basically, the way the build works is you have your Virtues line, which gives your Virtue skill 2 passive healing and also grants it to nearby allies. Honor is mostly focused on improving healing, giving a little bit of might, and also the trait Writ of Persistence. This is how the build heals for the most part: you heal through your symbols on the ground through hammer. That's where a good majority of your healing is coming from. You also grant might whenever you use up all of your Mantra skills, because the trait Weighty Terms applies slow, which in turn applies Stoic Demeanor. This build runs a few interesting skills. Advance! is usually something you give through Staff 3, but again, staff just doesn't have as much passive healing in this setup. Your only source of swiftness is Advance!. Your elite is a small source of quickness, but the main thing your elite is useful for is fury, because this build doesn't have any fury generation apart from your elite and your relic, the Relic of Altruism. You will grant fury and eight stacks of might whenever you use a healing skill. So your elite and the Relic of Altruism are how you keep up permanent fury uptime, which is really important. I would consider running staff, and I would also consider looking through and using your Tome skills. In these Firebrand builds I don't use them even though maybe I should. When I started making these, I viewed tomes as weapon swapping, and these builds don't have weapon swap. Again, all of your tomes are free to use. Some useful ones: Tome of Justice skill 3 is a pull. Tome of Resolve is your main healing tome. If you're taking a lot of damage and don't want to run staff, skill 5 and skill 4 are big healing skills, and skill 2 is a condi cleanse. Tome of Courage can grant stability, protection, Aegis, a reflect, and pulsing resistance. I would highly encourage you to either run staff and/or use your tome skills, but if you decide to do neither, I think hammer is your best option.

For utility skills, you only really have one flexible utility slot. Hammer 2 is a little mobility. Hammer 3 will proc your Stoic Demeanor, so if you want a little bit of resistance and might, you can use Hammer 3. Hammer 4 is CC. I put Sanctuary by default on this build. Sanctuary is a really strong CC skill, so I would probably just run it. Other strong options are Mantra of Lore for condi removal, Judge's Intervention to blink to an enemy, Merciful Intervention to blink to an ally and res them, Wall of Reflection, Hallowed Ground, Stand Your Ground!, Bane Signet if you need a little more CC, and Bow of Truth if you need more healing.

With these healer builds, it's hard because as a healer you need to be somewhat dynamic to what's happening in the fight, and there are going to be parts where people take a lot of damage and you need to pump out burst healing quickly. I'm trying to focus more on strong passive healing with these builds if you're going to be playing a low-intensity healer. I hope you enjoy the build. Thank you.
